questions.question: Describe the main differences between the n-Queens problem and the Subset-sum problem in the context of Backtracking. How do these differences affect the design of the algorithm for each problem? (15 marks)
questions.hint: Focus on the problem-solving approaches, time complexity, and space complexity

questions.question: How does the Branch-and-Bound method improve upon the naive recursive approach for solving the 0/1 Knapsack problem? Provide a step-by-step explanation of how the algorithm works, highlighting its key advantages and limitations. (15 marks)
questions.hint: Focus on the pruning technique, bounding function, and recursion

questions.question: Design an Approximation algorithm for solving the Bin Packing problem using a greedy approach. Calculate the time complexity of your algorithm and discuss its strengths and weaknesses in comparison to more optimal algorithms like First-Fit or Best-Fit. (10 marks)
questions.hint: Focus on algorithm design, time complexity, and comparison with other algorithms
